result:
I want to talk about how strangely beautiful it is that we’re all just walking around carrying entire universes inside us that no one else will ever fully see.

Every person you pass on the street has a secret list of songs that would destroy them if they heard them at the wrong moment. They have memories that still make them blush or cry in the shower years later. They have opinions about the smell of rain on hot pavement, or the exact way their childhood bedroom door sounded when it creaked. They have imaginary conversations with people who left them that they never got to finish in real life.

We’re all these messy, contradictory, half-finished symphonies pretending to be functional adults. Someone is listening to the same sad song on repeat right now while crying into their cereal. Someone else is rereading messages from someone who doesn’t love them anymore, just to feel something. Another person is secretly proud of the weird way they pronounce a certain word and will never tell a soul.

I think about how we spend so much of our lives performing competence while secretly being complete disasters in private. The CEO who still sleeps with the stuffed animal he had as a kid. The famous comedian who practices his smile in the mirror because it doesn’t come naturally. The tough guy who writes terrible poetry at 3 a.m. that he immediately deletes.

There’s something profoundly tender about that gap between who we are and who we pretend to be.

And yet we keep going. We make each other coffee. We send memes at the exact right moment. We hold doors and remember how someone takes their tea and show up even when we’re falling apart inside. We choose, over and over again, to be kind in small ways even though we’re all carrying these enormous invisible storms.

I don’t know. The world feels both much darker and much more luminous when you really think about it. We’re all these fragile miracles pretending we’re not.

Anyway, I just wanted to say that your particular storm (whatever it looks like today) is probably way more beautiful than you give it credit for. Even the ugly parts. Especially the ugly parts.

Thanks for giving me the space to ramble. I hope your day contains at least one small moment that feels like it was quietly meant for you.
